title: The Tango Lesson
introKo:
동네 문화센터에서 배우는 아주 건전한 탱고 강습 이야기.
…표면적으로는요.

quiz.q: Carlos는 탱고가 두 몸이 나누는 무엇이라고 했나요?

normal.text: Friday evening. You walk into the community center for your first tango class. The teacher, Carlos, waits in the middle of the room.
"Welcome," he says. "Tango is a conversation between two bodies. Tonight, you will learn to listen."
He takes your hand. "First, the hold. Come closer. Closer. In tango, there is no polite distance."

options.en: Like this? Is this close enough?
options.reaction: "Closer," he says. "When you can feel your partner breathe, you are ready."

options.en: Okay, but I'm a little nervous.
options.reaction: "Good," he smiles. "The body dances better when the heart beats fast."

normal.text: "My hand goes here," he says, and places it on your waist. "Firm, but gentle. You must trust the hand that holds you."
"Now the lead. I lead, you follow. Do not think. Feel my body, and let yours answer."

options.en: Then lead me. I'll go wherever you take me.
options.ko: 그럼 리드해 줘요. 어디로 데려가든 따라갈게요.
options.reaction: "You will feel it," he says. "My body will tell yours. Words are not needed."

options.en: What if I make a mistake?
options.reaction: "In tango there are no mistakes," he says. "Only moves we did not plan."

normal.text: You walk together across the floor. Step, step, pause. Slowly, your two bodies find one rhythm.
"Now hook your leg around mine," Carlos says. "Slowly. Yes — let them tangle. Do not hurry out of it."
The music slows. Your face is warm. "Good," Carlos says. "A cold dancer makes a cold tango. Heat is part of the dance."

options.reaction: "Ah, the dip," he smiles. "Beginners always rush to the ending. The good ones learn to take their time."

options.en: I don't want this song to end.
options.reaction: "Everyone says that on the first night," he laughs. "Tango does that to people."

normal.text: For the last move, Carlos dips you low and holds you there. "Give me your weight. I have you. Stay — I could hold this all night, but the music, sadly, cannot."

options.en: Don't lift me up yet. Start the next song.
options.ko: 아직 일으키지 말아요. 다음 곡을 틀어 줘요.
options.reaction: "Greedy," Carlos laughs, and does not move an inch. "I was hoping you would say that."

options.en: Thank you, Carlos. Same time next Friday?
options.ko: 고마워요, 카를로스. 다음 주 금요일 같은 시간에요?
options.reaction: "Of course," he says, lifting you up gently. "I will keep this hour just for you."

normal.text: You walk home with tired legs and a busy heart. Next week feels very far away. Once tango is in your body, it never leaves.

hard.text: It is Friday evening, and you push open the door of the community center, where your first tango class is about to begin. The teacher, Carlos, is waiting in the middle of the room.
"Welcome," he says with a small smile. "Tango is a conversation between two bodies. Tonight I will teach you how to listen."
He offers his hand, and you take it without thinking twice. "First, the hold. Come closer. Closer still. Tango is not a dance that respects polite distance."

options.en: Like this? Is this close enough for you?
options.reaction: "Closer," he says. "When you can feel your partner breathe, then you are ready to begin."

options.en: Okay, but I have to admit I'm a little nervous.
options.reaction: "Good," he smiles. "The body always dances better when the heart is beating fast."

hard.text: "My hand belongs here," he says, settling it on your waist. "Firm, but gentle. You must learn to trust the hand that holds you."
"Now the lead. I lead, and you follow. Do not think about the steps. Feel what my body is saying, and let yours answer."

options.en: Then lead me — I'll follow you wherever you decide to go.
options.ko: 그럼 리드해 줘요. 어디로 데려가든 따라갈게요.
options.reaction: "You will feel it," he says. "My body will tell yours everything. Words are rarely needed."

options.en: What happens if I make a mistake?
options.reaction: "In tango there are no mistakes," he says, "only moves that nobody planned."

hard.text: You move together across the floor. Step, step, pause. Little by little, your two bodies settle into a single rhythm.
"Now hook your leg around mine," Carlos says. "Slowly. Yes — let them tangle properly. Do not be in a hurry to untangle them."
The music slows, and you notice that your face is burning. "Good," Carlos says. "A cold dancer makes a cold tango. Heat is part of the dance."

options.en: Could we try the ending together?
options.reaction: "Ah, the dip," he smiles. "Beginners are always in a hurry to reach the ending. The good ones take their time."

options.en: Honestly, I don't want this song to end.
options.ko: 솔직히 이 노래가 안 끝났으면 좋겠어요.
options.reaction: "Everyone says that on their first night," he laughs. "Tango has that effect on people."

hard.text: For the final move, Carlos dips you low and holds you there. "Give me all your weight. I have you. Stay — I could hold this all night, but sadly, the music cannot."

options.en: Don't lift me up just yet. Let the next song begin.
options.ko: 아직 일으키지 말아요. 다음 곡이 시작되게 두세요.
options.reaction: "Greedy," Carlos laughs, without moving an inch. "I was quietly hoping you would say exactly that."

options.en: Thank you, Carlos. Shall we do the same time next Friday?
options.ko: 고마워요, 카를로스. 다음 주 금요일 같은 시간에 또 할까요?
options.reaction: "Of course," he says, raising you gently back to your feet. "I will keep this hour just for you."

hard.text: You walk home on legs that no longer feel like yours, with a heart that refuses to slow down. Next week seems terribly far away. Once tango is in your body, it never leaves.

evening.en: the end part of the day

community.en: people who live in the same area

tango.en: a slow partner dance from Argentina

middle.en: the center point of a place

welcome.en: a friendly hello to a guest

between.en: in the space of two things

listen.en: to pay attention with your ears

breathe.en: to take air in and out

beats.en: moves in a rhythm, like a heart

beating.en: moving in a rhythm, like a heart

waist.en: the middle part of your body

follow.en: to move where the leader goes

do.en: a helper word for questions and not

steps.en: moves of the feet in dance

know.en: to have something in your mind

move.en: one action of the body in dance

across.en: from one side to the other

floor.en: the flat surface you dance on

hook.en: to curl one thing around another

leg.en: the body part you walk with

legs.en: the body parts you walk with

dip.en: a dance move bending your partner back

does.en: form of do for he or she

longer.ko: 더 오래 (no longer: 더 이상 ~ 않다)

offers.en: holds out for you to take

reach.en: to arrive at, to get to

exactly.en: in a precise way, just right

monday.en: the first work day of the week

practice.en: to do again and again to learn

private.en: for one person only, not shared

arc.en: the shape of how something develops

begged.en: asked for something with strong feeling

blinking.en: opening and closing the eyes quickly

forward.en: toward the front; look forward means to await happily

goodbye.en: a word you say when you leave

goodnight.en: a friendly goodbye said at night

guessing.en: trying to know without being sure

hesitate.en: to wait because you are unsure

met.ko: 만났다 (meet의 과거형; meet one's match: 임자를 만나다)
met.en: came face to face (past of meet)

promise.en: words you will keep; a sign of good things

promising.en: showing good signs for the future

reads.en: has these words written on it

reluctant.en: slow to do; not fully willing

schedule.en: a plan of times for things

skip.en: to leave out; to not do

stamina.en: the energy to keep going a long time

beat.en: one steady unit of a rhythm

brave.en: not afraid; ready to face fear

closed.en: made smaller or shut (past of close)

finds.en: thinks something is a certain way

followed.en: went where the leader went (past of follow)

hello.en: a word you say when you meet someone

kind.ko: 종류 (the safest kind: 제일 안전한 부류)

noticed.en: saw and paid attention (past of notice)

second.en: a very short moment of time

sound.en: to seem a certain way when heard

spent.en: used time on something (past of spend)

weeks.en: of the week; belonging to a week

wink.hold: 탱고 기본 자세의 정식 명칭이에요. 수업 밖에서 이 단어는 그냥 '안는 것'이라, 어느 쪽 수업이었는지는 억양이 결정하죠.
wink.closer: '더 가까이'라는 춤 지시어예요. 반복해서 들으면 춤 얘기가 아닌 것처럼 들리기 시작하죠.
wink.lead: 춤에서 방향을 이끄는 역할이에요. 둘 사이의 주도권 얘기로도 읽힐 수 있고요.
wink.follow: 리드를 따라간다는 춤 용어예요. 몸을 상대에게 맡긴다는 뜻으로 들리면… 기분 탓일까요.
wink.waist: '허리에 손'은 탱고의 공식 기본 자세예요. 다만 'My hand belongs here'라는 말은, 문맥 없이 들으면 춤 설명처럼 안 들릴 뿐이죠.
wink.tangle: 다리가 엉키는 정식 탱고 스텝이에요. 문맥 없이 들으면 꽤 다른 장면이 떠오를 수 있죠.
wink.rhythm: 두 사람이 하나의 박자를 탄다는 춤 용어예요. 'We found our rhythm'은 춤 밖에서도 쓰는 표현인데, 어디서 쓰는지는 각자 아는 만큼만 떠올리기로 해요.
wink.dip: 상대를 뒤로 깊게 눕히는 마무리 동작이에요. '무게를 다 맡겨요'라는 말과 만나면, 상상은 각자의 몫이에요.
wink.dips: 상대를 뒤로 깊게 눕히는 마무리 동작이에요. '무게를 다 맡겨요'라는 말과 만나면, 상상은 각자의 몫이에요.
wink.greedy: 춤이 끝난 뒤 더 원한다는 뜻의 장난스러운 핀잔이에요. 무엇을 더 원하는지는 굳이 묻지 않기로 해요.

winkEn.hold: The official name for tango's basic embrace. Outside class, holding someone this close usually needs a different explanation.
winkEn.closer: A standard dance instruction. Repeat it enough times in a low voice and it stops sounding like one.
winkEn.lead: The dancer who decides where things go. Also a fair question about who is steering this whole evening.
winkEn.follow: Dance term for trusting the lead completely. Letting someone else move your body is, of course, strictly technical here.
winkEn.waist: Hand on the waist is textbook tango form. 'My hand belongs here,' heard out of context, is a much bolder claim.
winkEn.tangle: A legitimate tango step where legs intertwine. Described over the phone, it would raise a few questions.
winkEn.rhythm: Two people finding one beat — pure dance vocabulary. 'We found our rhythm' travels well beyond the dance floor, though.
winkEn.dip: The closing move where you lean back and give someone all your weight. Paired with 'I could hold this all night,' it does a lot of work.
winkEn.dips: The closing move where you lean back and give someone all your weight. Paired with 'I could hold this all night,' it does a lot of work.
winkEn.greedy: A playful scolding for wanting more after the dance ends. Exactly what you want more of is left politely unasked.

introEn:
A perfectly wholesome tango lesson at the community center.
...on the surface.

0000.name: 박자를 지키는 사람
0000.nameEn: The One Who Keeps the Beat
0000.en: You came in nervous, danced by the rules, and let the music decide when to stop. When Carlos asked, you booked next Friday and said a polite goodnight at the door. "A careful first dance," he says, "is a promise of a braver second one."
0000.ko: 긴장한 채로 들어와, 규칙대로 춤추고, 언제 멈출지는 음악에 맡겼어요. 카를로스가 묻자 다음 주 금요일을 예약하고 문 앞에서 점잖은 인사만 남겼죠. "조심스러운 첫 춤은," 그가 말해요. "더 용감한 두 번째 춤에 대한 약속이에요."

0001.name: 마지막에 아쉬워한 사람
0001.en: All night you asked the careful questions and followed the safe steps — until the dip, when you asked him not to lift you up yet. Carlos remembers only that last request. "The quiet ones," he tells the room, "always save the best move for last."
0001.ko: 밤새 조심스러운 질문만 하고 안전한 스텝만 따라갔어요 — 딥에서 아직 일으키지 말라고 하기 전까지는요. 카를로스가 기억하는 건 그 마지막 부탁 하나예요. "조용한 사람들은," 그가 홀에 대고 말해요. "늘 제일 좋은 동작을 마지막까지 아껴 둬요."

0010.name: 결말을 서두른 초보
0010.nameEn: The One Who Rushed the Finish
0010.en: You were shy in the hold and careful with the lead, but when the music heated up, you were the one asking to try the ending. Then, just as quickly, you thanked him and booked next Friday. "Beginners rush the dip," Carlos smiles. "I look forward to teaching you to wait."
0010.ko: 홀드에선 수줍고 리드에선 조심스러웠는데, 음악이 뜨거워지자 마지막 동작을 해보자고 먼저 청한 건 당신이었죠. 그러더니 또 금세 고맙다는 인사와 함께 다음 주 금요일을 예약했어요. "초보는 딥을 서둘러요," 카를로스가 웃어요. "기다리는 법을 가르칠 날이 기대되네요."

0011.name: 뒤로 갈수록 뜨거운 사람
0011.en: You started nervous and asked about mistakes, but by the end you rushed to the dip and would not let the last song die. Carlos knows this type well. "Cold at the door, warm by the final bar," he says. "You dance the way good tango is written."
0011.ko: 긴장한 채 실수 얘기부터 꺼냈지만, 끝에 가선 딥으로 달려가 마지막 곡을 놓아주지 않았죠. 카를로스는 이런 유형을 잘 알아요. "문 앞에선 차갑다가 마지막 마디에선 뜨거워지는," 그가 말해요. "좋은 탱고가 쓰이는 방식 그대로 추시네요."

0100.name: 리드에 몸을 맡긴 사람
0100.nameEn: The One Who Gave the Lead
0100.en: You were nervous and you wanted the song to last, but the moment he said follow me, you handed over your whole body without a question. Carlos noticed that one brave second. "You hesitate everywhere," he says, "except where it matters most."
0100.ko: 긴장했고 노래가 끝나지 않길 바랐지만, 그가 따라오라고 한 순간만큼은 아무 질문 없이 온몸을 맡겼죠. 카를로스는 그 용감한 1초를 놓치지 않았어요. "어디서나 망설이면서," 그가 말해요. "정작 제일 중요한 데서는 안 그러시네요."

0101.name: 결정적인 순간의 사람
0101.nameEn: The One for the Big Moments
0101.en: Careful with the small steps, but bold when it counted: you surrendered to his lead, and at the dip you begged the next song to start. Carlos calls you a dancer of big moments. "You save all your courage," he says, "for exactly the right ones."
0101.ko: 작은 스텝엔 조심스러웠지만 중요한 순간엔 과감했죠. 그의 리드에 몸을 맡겼고, 딥에서는 다음 곡을 틀어 달라고 졸랐으니까요. 카를로스는 당신을 큰 순간의 무용수라 불러요. "용기를 전부 아껴 뒀다가," 그가 말해요. "딱 맞는 순간에만 쓰시네요."

0110.name: 중반에 불붙는 사람
0110.en: You began shy, but somewhere between the lead and the heat you stopped hesitating — you followed him anywhere and chased the ending. By the dip you had turned polite again and simply booked next Friday. Carlos finds the whole arc very promising.
0110.ko: 수줍게 시작했는데, 리드와 열기 사이 어디쯤에서 망설임을 멈췄어요 — 어디든 그를 따라갔고 마지막 동작까지 쫓았죠. 딥에 이르러선 다시 점잖아져서 그저 다음 주 금요일을 예약했고요. 카를로스는 그 흐름 전체가 아주 기대된다네요.

0111.name: 한 박자 늦게 대담해진 사람
0111.nameEn: One Beat Behind, Then All In
0111.en: One nervous question at the start, and after that nothing could hold you back — you followed his lead, rushed the ending, and refused to let the last song end. "You only needed one beat to warm up," Carlos laughs. "From now on, I will skip the warmup."
0111.ko: 시작의 긴장된 질문 하나, 그 뒤로는 아무것도 당신을 막지 못했어요 — 리드를 따라가고, 마지막 동작으로 달려가고, 마지막 곡을 끝내 주지 않았죠. "몸 푸는 데 딱 한 박자면 됐네요," 카를로스가 웃어요. "앞으로는 준비 운동은 건너뛸게요."

1000.name: 첫인사만 뜨거운 사람
1000.en: You closed the distance the very first minute, then spent the rest of the night being wonderfully careful about everything else. Carlos calls it the safest kind of bold. He books you for next Friday anyway, still smiling about that opening line.
1000.ko: 첫 1분 만에 거리를 좁혀 놓고선, 남은 밤 내내 나머지 모든 것엔 지극히 조심스러웠죠. 카를로스는 그걸 세상에서 제일 안전한 대담함이라 불러요. 그래도 그 첫마디를 떠올리며 웃는 얼굴로 다음 주 금요일을 잡아 두네요.

1001.name: 처음과 끝만 과감한 사람
1001.en: You leaned in close at hello and refused to rise from the final dip — but everything in between you handled with careful, polite hands. "You are brave at the start and brave at the end," Carlos says. "The middle is where I will have my fun teaching you."
1001.ko: 인사할 때 바짝 다가섰고 마지막 딥에서 일어나길 거부했죠 — 그런데 그 사이의 모든 건 조심스럽고 점잖은 손길로 해냈어요. "시작에도 용감하고 끝에도 용감하시네요," 카를로스가 말해요. "그 사이를 가르치는 재미는 제 몫으로 남겨 두죠."

1010.name: 밀당의 명수
1010.en: Bold, then careful, then bold, then polite again — all night you kept Carlos guessing which one you really were. He loved every second of not knowing. "You dance like you are negotiating," he says at the door, and it did not sound like a complaint.
1010.ko: 과감했다가, 조심했다가, 다시 과감했다가, 끝엔 또 점잖아지고 — 밤새 카를로스에게 당신이 진짜 어떤 사람인지 헷갈리게 했죠. 그는 그 모름의 순간순간을 전부 사랑했어요. "꼭 협상하듯 추시네요," 문 앞에서 그가 말해요. 싫다는 말투는 아니었어요.

1011.name: 한 번만 빼고 다 과감한 사람
1011.en: You were close from hello, you chased the ending, and you would not let the last song die — only the lead made you pause and ask about mistakes. "One careful question in a whole bold night," Carlos says. "I will treasure it. It may be the only one I ever get."
1011.ko: 인사부터 가까웠고, 마지막 동작을 쫓았고, 마지막 곡을 죽게 두지 않았어요 — 오직 리드 앞에서만 멈칫하며 실수 얘길 물었죠. "과감한 밤 전체에 조심스러운 질문은 딱 하나," 카를로스가 말해요. "소중히 간직할게요. 앞으로 다시 못 들을지도 모르니까요."

1100.name: 손끝은 뜨겁고 발끝은 신중한 사람
1100.en: You pressed in close and handed him the lead without blinking, yet you still wanted the song to last and left with a polite goodnight. "Your body is braver than your schedule," Carlos says. "Next week, we work on the goodbye."
1100.ko: 바짝 다가서고 눈 하나 깜짝 않고 리드를 넘겼는데, 그러면서도 노래가 오래가길 바랐고 점잖은 인사와 함께 떠났죠. "몸이 일정표보다 용감하시네요," 카를로스가 말해요. "다음 주엔 작별 인사를 연습해 보죠."

1101.name: 노래만 짧다고 아쉬운 사람
1101.nameEn: The One Who Blamed the Song
1101.en: Close from the first bar, quick to give up the lead, and desperate to keep the night going — the only thing you asked to slow down was the music itself. "It is never the dancer," Carlos laughs. "It is always the poor, short song. We will find a longer one."
1101.ko: 첫 마디부터 가까웠고, 리드를 선뜻 넘겼고, 이 밤이 이어지길 간절히 바랐죠 — 유일하게 천천히 가 달라고 부탁한 건 음악뿐이었어요. "탓은 늘 무용수가 아니라," 카를로스가 웃어요. "늘 가여운 짧은 노래한테 있죠. 더 긴 곡을 찾아봐요."

1110.name: 거의 다 태우고 문 앞에서 식은 사람
1110.nameEn: All Fire, Then a Polite Door
1110.en: Bold in the hold, bold in the lead, bold rushing to the dip — and then, at the very last second, a calm thank-you and a booking for next Friday. Carlos stood at the door a while. "So much fire," he says, "and such a well-behaved goodbye."
1110.ko: 홀드에서 과감하고, 리드에서 과감하고, 딥으로 달려갈 때도 과감했는데 — 마지막 1초에 차분한 고맙다는 인사와 다음 주 금요일 예약이라니. 카를로스는 한참 문가에 서 있었어요. "그렇게 뜨거워 놓고," 그가 말해요. "작별 인사는 어쩜 이리 얌전한지."

1111.name: 음악보다 오래 남고 싶은 사람
1111.nameEn: The One Who Outlasts the Music
1111.en: Close from hello, surrendered to the lead, first to the dip, and the last to let it end — tonight Carlos finally met a partner who wanted one more song as badly as he did. He tears up next week's plan and writes a new one. The first line reads: private lessons, and bring stamina.
1111.ko: 인사부터 가까웠고, 리드에 몸을 맡겼고, 딥에 제일 먼저 닿았고, 끝을 제일 늦게 놓아준 사람 — 오늘 밤 카를로스는 드디어 한 곡 더를 자기만큼 간절히 원하는 파트너를 만났어요. 다음 주 계획을 찢고 새 계획을 써요. 첫 줄엔 이렇게 적혀 있죠. 개인 레슨, 그리고 체력 챙겨 올 것.
